title: plectranthus caninus subsp. flavovirens
intro: Plectranthus caninus subsp. flavovirens, also known as the Bushman's Spurflower or Flavovirens Plectranthus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is a shrub that is native to southern Africa, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving coughs and colds, and aiding in digestion, among others. In herbal preparations, Plectranthus caninus subsp. flavovirens is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
